title: Getting Started with hexmakR
text: Install hexmakR CRAN: install development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("hexmakR") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("cttir/hexmakR")

what: Your first hex sticker in 3 lines
title: Getting Started with hexmakR
text: ’s . default settings produce dark-themed sticker stats color palette atom icon.
code: library(hexmakR)  hex_sticker("mypackage", icon = "atom", theme = "stats")

title: Getting Started with hexmakR
text: Pass filename write transparent PNG directly: file correct hexb.aspect ratio (width / height = √3 / 2) transparent background, ready use README pkgdown site.
code: hex_sticker(   "mypackage",   icon     = "dna",   theme    = "genomics",   filename = "man/figures/logo.png" )

title: Getting Started with hexmakR
text: Preview theme interactively:
code: names(hexmakr_themes()) hexmakr_preview_theme("genomics", mode = "dark") hexmakr_preview_theme("genomics", mode = "light")

title: Getting Started with hexmakR
code: # All categories names(hexmakr_icons())  # Icons in a specific category hexmakr_icons("biology")

title: Getting Started with hexmakR
text: Override theme color individually:
code: hex_sticker(   "mypackage",   theme        = "stats",   mode         = "dark",   bg           = "#0D1117",   accent       = "#58A6FF",   text_color   = "#FFFFFF",   border_color = "#58A6FF",   sub_color    = "#8B949E" )

title: Getting Started with hexmakR
text: Available font shorthands: "mono", "courier", "consolas", "source_code", "fira_code", "serif", "times", "palatino", "sans", "helvetica", "roboto", "open_sans", "lato".
code: hex_sticker(   "mypackage",   font_family = "serif",   font_bold   = TRUE,   font_italic = TRUE,   font_size   = 14 )

title: Getting Started with hexmakR
text: full parameter reference: vignette("programmatic-usage") use interactive designer: vignette("shiny-app") just run hexmakr_app()

title: Getting Started with hexmakR
text: Portions package prepared assistance large language model tooling narrowly defined, non-authorial tasks: copyediting, prose smoothing, Markdown/LaTeX formatting, scaffolding boilerplate files (CI configs, build scripts), code refactoring. tools used Chat AI, LLM service KISSKI (GWDG), self-hosted Mistral Small (24B, Apache-2.0) run locally via Ollama ollamar R package — local inference , data sent third parties self-hosted model. scientific claims, methodological choices, analyses, interpretations, conclusions author’s . LLM-generated text incorporated without review revision, every reference verified DOI, arXiv ID, ISBN.

title: Programmatic Usage
text: function returns ggplot object, can chain additional ggplot2 layers:
code: library(hexmakR)  hex_sticker(   name         = "mypackage",   # Package name (required)   subtitle     = "subtitle",    # Shown below name   url          = "r-pkg.org",   # Shown at bottom   icon         = "atom",        # Built-in icon name   icon_image   = NULL,          # Path to custom PNG/JPG (overrides icon)   icon_size    = 1.0,           # Size multiplier (0.3 – 2.5)   theme        = "stats",       # Theme name   mode         = "dark",        # "dark" or "light"   bg           = NULL,          # Override background (#RRGGBB)   accent       = NULL,          # Override accent color   text_color   = NULL,          # Override text color   border_color = NULL,          # Override border color   sub_color    = NULL,          # Override subtitle/URL color   font_family  = "mono",        # Font shorthand or system font name   font_bold    = TRUE,   font_italic  = FALSE,   font_size    = 12,            # Base font size in pt   border_width = 2,             # Border width in mm   inner_border = TRUE,          # Draw inner accent ring   filename     = NULL,          # Auto-save if provided   width        = 600,           # Export width in pixels   dpi          = 300 ) library(ggplot2)  hex_sticker("mypackage") +   annotate("text", x = 0.5, y = 0.05,            label = "v0.1.0", color = "#64748B", size = 2.5)

title: Programmatic Usage
text: Generate stickers entire list packages:
code: packages <- list(   list(name = "pkgA", icon = "atom",     theme = "stats"),   list(name = "pkgB", icon = "dna",      theme = "genomics"),   list(name = "pkgC", icon = "bar_chart", theme = "viz") )  output_dir <- tempdir()  for (pkg in packages) {   hex_sticker(     name     = pkg$name,     icon     = pkg$icon,     theme    = pkg$theme,     filename = file.path(output_dir, paste0(pkg$name, ".png"))   ) }

title: Programmatic Usage
text: conventional location package logo man/figures/logo.png. placed , usethis pkgdown automatically pick . add README.md: _pkgdown.yml:
code: # Inside your package directory: hex_sticker( name = "mypackage", subtitle = "Does something great", url = "github.com/you/mypackage", icon = "gear", theme = "tech", filename = "man/figures/logo.png" ) template: bootstrap: 5 navbar: title: image: man/figures/logo.png href: index.html

title: Programmatic Usage
code: s <- hex_sticker("mypackage", theme = "tidy")  # PNG (transparent background) save_hex(s, "logo.png", width = 600, dpi = 300)  # SVG (requires svglite) save_hex_svg(s, "logo.svg", width = 2)

title: Programmatic Usage
code: themes <- hexmakr_themes() for (nm in names(themes)) {   cat(nm, "—", themes[[nm]]$label, "\n") }

title: Programmatic Usage
code: icons <- hexmakr_icons() for (cat_name in names(icons)) {   icon_names <- names(icons[[cat_name]])   cat(cat_name, "(", length(icon_names), "):", paste(icon_names, collapse = ", "), "\n") }

title: Interactive Shiny App
text: opens hexmakR Shiny designer default browser (RStudio Viewer pane).
code: library(hexmakR) hexmakr_app()

title: Interactive Shiny App
text: app divided two panels:

title: Interactive Shiny App
text: hex sticker updates real time change control. Export PNG button downloads 600 × 693 px transparent PNG.

title: Interactive Shiny App
text: controls organized five sections:

title: Interactive Shiny App
text: Five color pickers let override individual theme color: Background, Accent, Text, Border, Subtitle.

title: Interactive Shiny App
text: Start theme — pick theme Theme section matches package’s domain. Pick icon — use Built-Icon dropdown upload custom logo. Adjust text — set package name; add subtitle fits chosen font size. Fine-tune colors — Custom Colors section lets tweak individual colors without switching themes. Export — click Export PNG button. file named package.

title: Interactive Shiny App
text: Place downloaded PNG man/figures/logo.png package directory add README: See vignette("programmatic-usage") details integrating sticker usethis pkgdown.

title: Authors and Citation
text: R. Heller. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Heller R (2026). hexmakR: Interactive Hex Sticker Generator R Packages. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/cttir/hexmakR.
code: @Manual{,   title = {hexmakR: Interactive Hex Sticker Generator for R Packages},   author = {R. Heller},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/cttir/hexmakR}, }

title: Interactive Hex Sticker Generator for R Packages
text: hexmakR interactive hex sticker generator R packages. Design publication-ready hexagon stickers visually Shiny app programmatically via R functions. Comes 19 curated color themes (dark + light), 46 built-scientific technical icons, custom image support, font selection, transparent PNG export.

what: Standing on the shoulders of hexSticker
title: Interactive Hex Sticker Generator for R Packages
text: excellent hexSticker package Guangchuang Yu original widely used hex sticker generator R ecosystem. Available CRAN, provides powerful programmatic API built ggplot2 lets embed R plot, lattice graphic, ggplot, image file hex sticker — hundreds R package stickers created . hexmakR replacement hexSticker — complement. want embed actual R plot (base, lattice, ggplot2) sticker, hexSticker right tool. excels code-first, reproducible sticker generation full control subplot positioning. hexmakR fills different niche. focuses : Interactive visual design — Shiny app see changes live export ’re happy, code required curated theme library — 19 domain-specific color palettes (Statistics, Bioinformatics, Genomics, Pharma, CRAN, Bioconductor, Tidyverse, ) one-click dark/light switching built-icon library — 46 hand-drawn scientific technical icons across 7 categories, don’t need external image files Font selection — choose 13 fonts bold/italic toggles directly UI Zero--sticker speed — designed case want good-looking hex sticker minute without writing code recommend trying using whichever fits workflow. many users, best approach prototype hexmakR’s Shiny app fine-tune programmatically — either package.

title: Create a Hex Sticker — hex_sticker
text: Generates publication-ready hexagon sticker R package. Returns ggplot object can printed, customised +, saved save_hex().

title: Create a Hex Sticker — hex_sticker
code: hex_sticker(   name = "package",   subtitle = NULL,   url = NULL,   icon = NULL,   icon_image = NULL,   icon_size = 1,   theme = "stats",   mode = "dark",   bg = NULL,   accent = NULL,   text_color = NULL,   border_color = NULL,   sub_color = NULL,   font_family = "mono",   font_bold = TRUE,   font_italic = FALSE,   font_size = 12,   border_width = 2,   inner_border = TRUE,   filename = NULL,   width = 600,   dpi = 300 )

title: Create a Hex Sticker — hex_sticker
text: name Character. package name displayed sticker. subtitle Character NULL. Optional subtitle shown package name. url Character NULL. Optional URL shown near bottom sticker. icon Character NULL. Name built-icon. See hexmakr_icons() valid values. Ignored icon_image provided. icon_image Character NULL. Path custom image file (PNG JPEG). provided, overrides icon. icon_size Numeric. Size multiplier icon (default 1.0). theme Character. Theme name. One names(hexmakr_themes()). Default "stats". mode Character. Color mode, either "dark" (default) "light". bg Character NULL. Override background color (hex string, e.g. "#1B2838"). accent Character NULL. Override accent color. text_color Character NULL. Override text color. border_color Character NULL. Override border color. sub_color Character NULL. Override subtitle/URL color. font_family Character. Font shorthand (e.g. "mono", "serif", "sans") system font name. Default "mono". font_bold Logical. Use bold weight. Default TRUE. font_italic Logical. Use italic style. Default FALSE. font_size Numeric. Base font size pt package name. Default 12. border_width Numeric. Border line width mm. Default 2. inner_border Logical. Draw thin inner accent ring. Default TRUE. filename Character NULL. provided, save sticker transparent PNG path using save_hex(). width Integer. Export width pixels (height derived hex aspect ratio). Default 600. dpi Numeric. Resolution PNG export. Default 300.

title: Create a Hex Sticker — hex_sticker
text: ggplot object (invisibly filename NULL).

title: Create a Hex Sticker — hex_sticker
text: Create hex sticker

title: Create a Hex Sticker — hex_sticker
code: # \donttest{ # Minimal sticker hex_sticker("mypackage")   # With icon and theme hex_sticker("mypackage", icon = "dna", theme = "genomics")   # Save to file tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png") hex_sticker("mypackage", icon = "atom", theme = "stats", filename = tmp) file.exists(tmp) #> [1] TRUE # }

what: hexmakR: Interactive Hex Sticker Generator for R Packages — hexmakR-package
title: hexmakR: Interactive Hex Sticker Generator for R Packages — hexmakR-package
text: Create publication-ready hexagon stickers R packages interactively via Shiny app programmatically via R functions. Comes 19 curated color themes (dark + light variants), 46 built-scientific technical icons across 7 categories, custom image upload support, 13 font options, transparent PNG export.

title: hexmakR: Interactive Hex Sticker Generator for R Packages — hexmakR-package
text: hex_sticker() –programmatic hex sticker creation; returns ggplot object. hexmakr_app() –launch interactive Shiny designer. hexmakr_themes() –list 19 built-themes. hexmakr_icons() –list 46 built-icons. save_hex() –save sticker transparent PNG. save_hex_svg() –save sticker SVG (requires svglite).

title: hexmakR: Interactive Hex Sticker Generator for R Packages — hexmakR-package
text: Maintainer: Raban Heller raban.heller@uni-ulm.de (ORCID)

what: Launch Interactive Hex Sticker Designer — hexmakr_app
title: Launch Interactive Hex Sticker Designer — hexmakr_app
text: Opens interactive Shiny app designing hex stickers visually. app provides live preview, theme selection, icon picking, custom color overrides, PNG export.

title: Launch Interactive Hex Sticker Designer — hexmakr_app
code: hexmakr_app(...)

title: Launch Interactive Hex Sticker Designer — hexmakr_app
text: ... Additional arguments passed shiny::runApp().

title: Launch Interactive Hex Sticker Designer — hexmakr_app
text: return; runs Shiny app.

title: Launch Interactive Hex Sticker Designer — hexmakr_app
text: Launch hexmakR Shiny App

title: Launch Interactive Hex Sticker Designer — hexmakr_app
code: if (interactive()) {   hexmakr_app() }

title: List Available Icons — hexmakr_icons
text: Returns built-icon library named list. Icons organized category. icon entry contains label draw function.

title: List Available Icons — hexmakr_icons
code: hexmakr_icons(category = NULL)

title: List Available Icons — hexmakr_icons
text: category Character NULL. NULL (default), returns categories. Otherwise, one "science", "biology", "stats", "medical", "tech", "shapes", "fun".

title: List Available Icons — hexmakr_icons
text: named list icon entries (label draw), named list categories category = NULL.

title: List Available Icons — hexmakr_icons
text: List available icons

title: List Available Icons — hexmakr_icons
code: icons <- hexmakr_icons() names(icons) #> [1] "science" "biology" "stats"   "medical" "tech"    "shapes"  "fun"     hexmakr_icons("science") #> $atom #> $atom$label #> [1] "Atom" #>  #> $atom$draw #> function (x, y, size, color)  #> { #>     r_nucleus <- size * 0.06 #>     r_orbit <- size * 0.28 #>     grid::grid.circle(x = x, y = y, r = r_nucleus, gp = grid::gpar(fill = color,  #>         col = color)) #>     for (angle in c(0, 60, 120)) { #>         rad <- angle * pi/180 #>         cos_a <- cos(rad) #>         sin_a <- sin(rad) #>         xs <- x + r_orbit * cos(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 100)) *  #>             cos_a - r_orbit * 0.4 * sin(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 100)) *  #>             sin_a #>         ys <- y + r_orbit * sin(seq(0, 2 * pi, length.out = 100)) *  #>             cos_a * 0.4 + r_orbit * 0.4 * cos(seq(0, 2 * pi,  #>             length.out = 100)) * sin_a #>         grid::grid.lines(x = xs, y = ys, gp = grid::gpar(col = color,  #>             lwd = size * 4, lty = 1)) #>     } #> } #> <bytecode: 0x562bc290e6a0> #> <environment: namespace:hexmakR> #>  #>  #> $flask #> $flask$label #> [1] "Flask" #>  #> $flask$draw #> function (x, y, size, color)  #> { #>     grid::grid.rect(x = x, y = y + size * 0.1, width = size *  #>         0.14, height = size * 0.22, gp = grid::gpar(fill = color,  #>         col = color)) #>     bx <- c(x - size * 0.28, x + size * 0.28, x + size * 0.12,  #>         x - size * 0.12) #>     by <- c(y - size * 0.25, y - size * 0.25, y + size * 0, y +  #>         size * 0) #>     grid::grid.polygon(x = bx, y = by, gp = grid::gpar(fill = color,  #>         col = color)) #>     grid::grid.rect(x = x, y = y + size * 0.22, width = size *  #>         0.2, height = size * 0.04, gp = grid::gpar(fill = color,  #>         col = color)) #> } #> <bytecode: 0x562bc290f718> #> <environment: namespace:hexmakR> #>  #>  #> $microscope #> $microscope$label #> [1] "Microscope" #>  #> $microscope$draw #> function (x, y, size, color)  #> { #>     grid::grid.rect(x = x, y = y - size * 0.26, width = size *  #>         0.4, height = size * 0.06, gp = grid::gpar(fill = color,  #>         col = color)) #>     grid::grid.rect(x = x, y = y - size * 0.06, width = size *  #>         0.07, height = size * 0.38, gp = grid::gpar(fill = color,  #>         col = color)) #>     grid::grid.rect(x = x + size * 0.08, y = y + size * 0.18,  #>         width = size * 0.22, height = size * 0.05, gp = grid::gpar(fill = color,  #>             col = color)) #>     grid::grid.circle(x = x + size * 0.19, y = y + size * 0.22,  #>         r = size * 0.05, gp = grid::gpar(fill = color, col = color)) #>     grid::grid.rect(x = x, y = y + size * 0.03, width = size *  #>         0.05, height = size * 0.12, gp = grid::gpar(fill = color,  #>         col = color)) #> } #> <bytecode: 0x562bc2910fe0> #> <environment: namespace:hexmakR> #>  #>  #> $molecule #> $molecule$label #> [1] "Molecule" #>  #> $molecule$draw #> function (x, y, size, color)  #> { #>     positions <- list(c(0, 0), c(0.22, 0.18), c(-0.22, 0.18),  #>         c(0.22, -0.18), c(-0.22, -0.18)) #>     for (i in 2:5) { #>         grid::grid.lines(x = c(x, x + positions[[i]][1] * size),  #>             y = c(y, y + positions[[i]][2] * size), gp = grid::gpar(col = color,  #>                 lwd = size * 3)) #>     } #>     grid::grid.circle(x = x, y = y, r = size * 0.08, gp = grid::gpar(fill = color,  #>         col = color)) #>     for (i in 2:5) { #>         grid::grid.circle(x = x + positions[[i]][1] * size, y = y +  #>             positions[[i]][2] * size, r = size * 0.06, gp = grid::gpar(fill = color,  #>             col = color)) #>     } #> } #> <bytecode: 0x562bc2916198> #> <environment: namespace:hexmakR> #>  #>  #> $magnet #> $magnet$label #> [1] "Magnet" #>  #> $magnet$draw #> function (x, y, size, color)  #> { #>     theta <- seq(0, pi, length.out = 60) #>     r_out <- size * 0.26 #>     r_in <- size * 0.14 #>     xo <- x + r_out * cos(theta) #>     yo <- y + r_out * sin(theta) #>     xi <- rev(x + r_in * cos(theta)) #>     yi <- rev(y + r_in * sin(theta)) #>     px <- c(xo, xi) #>     py <- c(yo, yi) #>     grid::grid.polygon(x = px, y = py, gp = grid::gpar(fill = color,  #>         col = color)) #>     grid::grid.rect(x = x - r_out * 0.7, y = y - size * 0.06,  #>         width = (r_out - r_in), height = size * 0.12, gp = grid::gpar(fill = color,  #>             col = color)) #>     grid::grid.rect(x = x + r_out * 0.7, y = y - size * 0.06,  #>         width = (r_out - r_in), height = size * 0.12, gp = grid::gpar(fill = color,  #>             col = color)) #> } #> <bytecode: 0x562bc2917860> #> <environment: namespace:hexmakR> #>  #>

title: Save a Hex Sticker — save_hex
text: Renders saves hex sticker ggplot object PNG file transparent background correct hexb.aspect ratio (width / height = sqrt(3) / 2).

title: Save a Hex Sticker — save_hex
code: save_hex(sticker, filename, width = 600, dpi = 300)

title: Save a Hex Sticker — save_hex
text: sticker ggplot object returned hex_sticker(). filename Character. Output file path. Must end .png. width Integer. Width pixels. Default 600. dpi Numeric. Resolution. Default 300.

title: Save a Hex Sticker — save_hex
text: filename path, invisibly.

title: Save a Hex Sticker — save_hex
text: Save hex sticker PNG file

title: Save a Hex Sticker — save_hex
code: # \donttest{ s <- hex_sticker("mypackage", icon = "atom", theme = "stats") tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".png") save_hex(s, tmp) file.exists(tmp) #> [1] TRUE # }

title: hexmakR 0.1.0
text: Initial release 19 curated color themes dark light variants 46 built-icons across 7 categories (science, biology, data/stats, medical, tech/code, shapes, fun) Custom image upload support (PNG, JPG, SVG) 13 font options bold/italic toggles Interactive Shiny app visual sticker design Programmatic API scripted/batch sticker creation Export transparent PNG configurable resolution Full roxygen2 documentation 3 vignettes
